title: BSN ergonomics
author: johanneshorner
body:
- The crate's current API is very `Bundle` focused
- `Binding`, `Scale` and others do not implement `Default` and therefore do not automatically implement `FromTemplate`

These points (and likely more) currently make it kinda unergonomic to use BEI with `bsn!`. Are there any plans to improve this, or is using it with `bsn!` not intended anyway?

title: Rename `TimeKind::Virtual`
author: Shatur
body: The name is a bit confusing since it's not always [`Virtual`](https://docs.rs/bevy/latest/bevy/prelude/struct.Virtual.html), it's sometimes [`Fixed`](https://docs.rs/bevy/latest/bevy/prelude/struct.Fixed.html). Maybe `Default` or `Current` would be a better name as it named in the [docs](https://docs.rs/bevy/latest/bevy/prelude/struct.Time.html).

title: Change `DeltaScale` to use `TimeKind::Virtual` as default
author: jayhwkns
body:
I think it may be more practical to use Virtual time as the default for `DeltaScale`. 

Picture a typical use case: you are using `DeltaScale::default()` with the left stick to accelerate the player. The change in velocity is scaled by real time, but we'll say that the game has a modifier that's slowing the game down to 0.5x speed. The player's movement speed will be affected, but they will accelerate much 2x faster in in-game time.

Or in possibly a more common case, the user doesn't have a check to see if the game is paused in the system that handles movement input. They hold left on the stick while in the pause screen and unpause the game. They accelerate immediately without any checks.

I haven't ran into these issues personally, but I can definitely picture someone spending a while trying to find the issue if this were to happen to them.

I agree that the default `TimeKind` being `Real` makes sense in a lot of cases. You might want the player to still just hold a button for the same amount of real time to fire and action regardless of if the game is slowed down, but for DeltaScale I believe it should be handled differently.

Alternatively, we could move this choice onto the user explicitly by replacing the `Default` implementation with functions like `DeltaScale::real()` and `DeltaScale::virtual()`.

title: Helper for fixed timestep boilerplate (`did_fixed_timestep_run_this_frame`)
author: jayhwkns
body:
As discussed in #323, it is best practice to handle input by using a fixed timestep as was done in [this example](https://bevy.org/examples/movement/physics-in-fixed-timestep/) and [bevy_ahoy](https://github.com/janhohenheim/bevy_ahoy). This unfortunately introduces a lot of repetitive boilerplate code. Seeing as making a character controller is a common use-case for this crate, it may be productive to make this a library feature.

Essentially, we could add something like [this module from bevy_ahoy](https://github.com/janhohenheim/bevy_ahoy/blob/main/src/fixed_update_utils.rs) so that the users of the crate don't have to implement it all from scratch. It is the key to this line from the fixed timestep physics example, which prevents input from fixed timesteps being wiped when they shouldn't be:

```rust
App::new()
  // ...
  .add_systems(RunFixedMainLoop, clear_input.run_if(did_fixed_timestep_run_this_frame))
```

title: Keyboard binding only supports `KeyCode` and not `Key`
author: newclarityex
body: `KeyCode` is useful for positional keyboard bindings, but not logical keyboard bindings, which can be useful for implementing hotkeys like Ctrl + Z. LogicalKeyboard can be added to support `Key` bindings.

title: Remove `ActiveInStates::matches` in the next release
author: Shatur
body: It's not useful for users and I'd like to have a private method with this name that accepts `Option` instead.

title: State-context support for SubStates
author: mnmaita
body:
I wanted to implement [state-context integration](https://docs.rs/bevy_enhanced_input/latest/bevy_enhanced_input/state/index.html) in my project but I soon realized that it won't work properly with `SubStates`. I get a panic, probably because the observer that syncs the `ContextActivity`, `sync_on_insert`, expects the state resource to exist, and this is not always true with `SubStates`.

We could make the States resource param optional in `sync_on_insert` but I think there might be at least one case where it'll be `None` when `sync_on_insert` observer triggers. I'm thinking a sub-state whose source State was not yet set, but `ActiveInStates` was just inserted to some entity. To expand this example, let's say we have an entity with some bindings that gets spawned in a system running `On(State::First)` with an `ActiveInStates<Context, SubState::SomeVariant>` component. If said `SubStates`' source is `State::Second` it sounds it could be an issue, because once the State changes to `State::Second` and `SubStates` changes too, `sync_on_insert` will not necessarily be triggered again.

We can always add some warning logs if issues like these arise, but there might be better ways to support this.

title: Improve examples
author: Shatur
body:
We need to make the following changes to our examples to better teach users:
- [x] Write a proper character controller example instead of the current one. See https://bevy.org/examples/movement/physics-in-fixed-timestep/ for more details. To put it short, it should run in `FixedUpdate` and accumulate input. Check how [`bevy_ahoy` does it](https://github.com/janhohenheim/bevy_ahoy/blob/90beac8e05a280acf2d61a654bf0fecbf3404b50/src/input.rs#L1).
- [ ] Add `pull_style_api` that mirrors `basic_action_management`, but uses pull-style API.
- [x] ~~Rework `local_multiplayer` showcases a character controller that runs in `Update`, which isn't nice (see the link above). Let's change it into a snake game with 2 snakes (to showcase local multiplayer). In this kind of game we can mention that it's ok to use `Update`. It also sounds fun 😅(offtopic, but I also recently discovered a [snake MMO via SSH](https://eieio.games/blog/secure-massively-multiplayer-snake/)).~~ Adjust `local_multiplayer` as well.

Each change could be done in a separate PR. Contributions are welcome. I think it's a good opportunity for new contributors to play with the crate and Bevy in general 🙂

title: Movement in examples is framerate-dependent
author: ChristopherBiscardi
body:
The examples are good for showing usage, but promote using framerate-dependent application of input, which matters for things like movement.

A good example is [bevy_action_management](https://github.com/simgine/bevy_enhanced_input/blob/18b29c06b42ebd7a384a77735b49d665856141ee/examples/basic_action_management.rs#L103), which results in faster or slower movement depending on the framerate.

This isn't mentioned in the examples or the docs as far as I can tell, so users who use the examples will all end up with this bug unless they test on monitors with different framerates.

Given that the addition of a "controller" layer is basically required (as far as I know), so that movement is applied in `FixedUpdate`, it seems like this information deserves a spot in the docs and applying movement directly in action observers should never be done in examples.

title: Consider changing `ActionState` to `ConditionState` to better represent what state it is tracking.
author: Tsudico
body:
Recent discussion on discord involved some confusion in regards to how to properly use `ActionState` in an `InputCondition`. By having the name of `ActionState` there may be confusion in thinking that it directly affects the state of the `Action` the `InputCondition` is applied to instead of being the state of the `InputCondition` itself.

As additional evidence the name should be changed, the documentation for each enumeration of `ActionState` specifically mentions the Condition's state:
- `ActionState::None`: Condition is not triggered.
- `ActionState::Ongoing`: Condition has started triggering, but has not yet finished.
- `ActionState::Fired`: The condition has been met.

Further improvements to help with clarity might include changing the individual enumerated state names to be more distinct from `ActionEvents`. A possible alternative is:
- `ConditionState::Inactive`: Condition is not triggered.
- `ConditionState::Progressing`: Condition has some requirements met, but not all.
- `ConditionState::Activated`: The condition has been met.

title: Consider adding an event that is triggered when an action mock expires
author: mgi388

title: Add `Toggle` condition
author: mgi388
body:
I've made a `Toggle` condition. Here's the gist: https://gist.github.com/mgi388/da68cb3e8fe42ee54326f9bb381a1966. 

See [Discord](https://discord.com/channels/691052431525675048/1297361733886677036/1457691842429587538) for more context.

Why? A toggle condition is useful for actions that you want to remain "on" after the player triggers them. For example, if the player presses `F` you can use a toggle to make the `SelectingFireTarget` action remain "on", and then when they press `F` again the action turns "off".

I haven't PRd this because a) I haven't added an updated example and b) I haven't ironed out kinks in using this in my own code. When I say kinks, I don't mean to say the implementation above is full of bugs because it's pretty a simple condition. It's just that I programmatically toggle the condition on and off and I haven't 100% ironed out all the bugs in my own code around all the other actions that need to play nicely with this.

Someone else is welcome to PR it, but I wanted to create the issue to share the gist publicly and also track the feature request.

title: Add `try_` versions of `EntityCommands` `mock` and `mock_once`
author: mgi388
body:
See [Discord](https://discord.com/channels/691052431525675048/1297361733886677036/1468565228538171412) for context.

Why? It's easy to experience the panic e.g., `entity 462v0 has no Actions<MyInputContext>` when you're spawning and despawning contexts (e.g., when using context layering) intertwined with mocking. 

Even if I set my Bevy error handler to warn, not panic, I'd still see warning logs telling me that a context doesn't exist, but there's nothing I can do about it, so having `try_` would be useful to callers can indicate they don't care if it fails.

title: Call `register_type` for `ActionOf`, `Actions`, `ContextActivity` and `ContextPriority`
author: mgi388

title: MouseButton::Right seems not to work on Bevy0.18/Bei 0.22
author: PatrickChodowski

title: `ModKeys` serializes as raw integer instead of human-readable string
author: jonas-meyer
body:
## Problem

When serializing `Binding` with the `serialize` feature enabled, `ModKeys` serializes as a raw `u8` integer instead of a human-readable string.

**Current behavior:**

```json
{
  "Keyboard": {
    "key": "KeyZ",
    "mod_keys": 1
  }
}
```

**Wanted behavior:**

```json
{
  "Keyboard": {
    "key": "KeyZ",
    "mod_keys": "CONTROL"
  }
}
```

Or for combined modifiers:

```json
{
  "mod_keys": "CONTROL | SHIFT"
}
```

## Root Cause

`ModKeys` currently derives `Serialize`/`Deserialize` directly on the newtype wrapper:

```rust
#[cfg_attr(feature = "serialize", derive(Serialize, Deserialize))]
pub struct ModKeys(u8);
```

This bypasses bitflags' serde integration which provides human-readable serialization via `bitflags::serde::{serialize, deserialize}`.

## Why This Matters

For config files (RON, JSON, YAML), users need to know magic numbers:
- `1` = CONTROL
- `2` = SHIFT  
- `3` = CONTROL + SHIFT
- `4` = ALT
- etc.

This is error-prone and not user-friendly for hand-edited config files.

## Proposed Solution

Replace the derive with manual implementations:

```rust
#[cfg(feature = "serialize")]
impl Serialize for ModKeys {
    fn serialize<S: Serializer>(&self, serializer: S) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error> {
        bitflags::serde::serialize(self, serializer)
    }
}

#[cfg(feature = "serialize")]
impl<'de> Deserialize<'de> for ModKeys {
    fn deserialize<D: Deserializer<'de>>(deserializer: D) -> Result<Self, D::Error> {
        bitflags::serde::deserialize(deserializer)
    }
}
```

## Why Not Struct Mode?

I tested using `bitflags!` struct mode with `#[serde(transparent)]`, which would give automatic human-readable serde. However, this breaks Bevy's `Reflect` derive because bitflags generates an internal `InternalBitFlags` type that doesn't implement reflection traits.

The manual impl approach is the same pattern used by [wgpu](https://github.com/gfx-rs/wgpu/blob/trunk/wgpu-types/src/features.rs#L181-L198).

## Compatibility

- **Binary formats** (bincode, etc.): Still serialize as raw bits (unchanged)
- **Human-readable formats** (JSON, RON, YAML): Now serialize as strings
- **Parsing**: Backwards compatible - can still parse integers

I can submit a PR if this approach sounds good.

title: Add State-Context Integration
author: jonas-meyer

title: Chord behavior differs to VS Code which threw me off
author: mgi388
body: One thing that just tripped me up is `Chord` vs `Combo`. In VS Code, a chord is where I press, say, `cmd+k`, then wait a bit, then press something else and VS Code picks up the action (see screenshot). However, in the `all_conditions.rs` example, the `Chord` action only fires if you press `8` and `9` _at the same time_. This tripped me up because of the difference to VS Code. I don't really mind, because I suspect BEI is using UE terms, but just thought I'd share in case anyone else comes across this. If we discover every input system and app out there prefers `Chord` to mean what it means in VS Code, then _maybe_ you might change it, but if not, it may just be worth keeping in mind and/or documenting further. At least for now, this issue is just opened to capture the papercut, I wouldn't rename anything after just one experience report. Originally reported in [Discord](https://discord.com/channels/691052431525675048/1297361733886677036/1457587187532169288).

title: Discord link should be invite. Unable to join.
author: Soupborsh
body:
Hello, I want to get help and want to join a discord channel to ask a question, however the link to Discord channel in the README and in crates.io page is "https://discord.com/channels/691052431525675048/1297361733886677036". This is incorrect and it should be in a form like these examples: "https://discord.gg/wumpus-friends", "https://discord.gg/hTKzmak", "hTKzmak".

I suppose the current link will only work for already members of the channel, but will not work for non-members.

Fix: create actual invite link and replace the current one with it.


P.S.: I wanted to ask how to properly get Gamepad stick input as Vec2? There seem to be only single axis ones, but I need both as a vector.
